episodes.title: Leaving Christianity Feels Like Escaping a Cult
episodes.description:
I was literally trained to spread the gospel or die trying, which is exactly why leaving Christianity finally feels less like a "loss of faith" and more like escaping a cult. For generations, my family has been part of a mission organization where the mission was sacred and the people were expendable.

🫀 Lil Patreon to support the ongoing production of these vids 🥹
https://www.patreon.com/c/rewritingeden

☕️ Help me stay caffeinated while editing after work
https://ko-fi.com/rewritingeden

🔗 Instagram
https://www.instagram.com/rewriting.eden

0:00 The Exhaustion of Thinking for Myself
1:10 Generations of "Funneling" Into Missions
1:53 Jesus as the Cult Leader?
3:10 Growing Up in the "Ideal" Doctrine
3:40 Abeka Homeschooling & Information Control
4:52 The Isolation of the Indonesia Bubble
6:42 Leader Worship & the "Filthy Rags" Doctrine
7:52 Spread the Gospel or Die Trying (The Hostage Booklet)
9:01 Thought Policing: "Doubt is Satan"
10:48 Fear Tactics: Hell & Divine Punishment
12:47 Us vs. Them: Saved vs. Heathen
13:34 Purity Culture & the Obsession with Virginity
14:07 Divorce, LGBTQ+, and "Heterosexual Heaven"
15:50 The Performance: Evangelism as Social Currency
16:55 Protecting the Brand: The Dark Side of Missions
18:43 Reputation Over Victims: The Institutional Trap
20:30 The Exit: Death by a Thousand Cuts
21:22 Vertigo & Decision Paralysis
22:30 Delayed Adolescence: Pop Culture & Britney Spears
23:48 Mapping the BITE Model: Was It a Cult?
25:30 Waking Up Without Fear
